name: braa
title: braa
description:
Mass SNMP scanner
Braa is a mass snmp scanner. The intended usage of such a tool is of course
making SNMP queries - but unlike snmpget or snmpwalk from net-snmp, it is able
to query dozens or hundreds of hosts simultaneously, and in a single process.
Thus, it consumes very few system resources and does the scanning VERY fast.
.
Braa implements its OWN snmp stack, so it does NOT need any SNMP libraries
like net-snmp. The implementation is very dirty, supports only several data
types, and in any case cannot be stated 'standard-conforming'! It was designed
to be fast, and it is fast. For this reason (well, and also because of author
laziness ;), there is no ASN.1 parser in braa - the user HAVE to know the
numerical values of OID's
(for instance .1.3.6.1.2.1.1.5.0 instead of system.sysName.0).

arguments.description: Quit after <time> seconds, independent on what happens.
arguments.raw: -a  Quit after <time> seconds, independent on what happens.

arguments.description: Wait <s> microseconds after sending each packet.
arguments.raw: -d  Wait <s> microseconds after sending each packet.

arguments.description: Load queries from file <file> (one by line).
arguments.raw: -f  Load queries from file <file> (one by line).

arguments.description: Show short summary after doing all queries.
arguments.raw: -v  Show short summary after doing all queries.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use braa because braa - Mass SNMP scanner.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use braa as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `braa  [-h]  [-2] [-v] [-t <s>] [-f <file] [-a <time>] [-r <retries>] [-d <delay>] [querylist1]` as documented upstream for braa.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `braa public@$KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST:161:.1.3.6.*` as documented upstream for braa.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `braa $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST-$KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST:.$KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST.$KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST.0` as documented upstream for braa.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `braa private@$KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST:.$KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST.$KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST.0=sMy network` as documented upstream for braa.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair braa with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `braa --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install braa. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `braa  [-h]  [-2] [-v] [-t <s>] [-f <file] [-a <time>] [-r <retries>] [-d <delay>] [querylist1]`. Further invocations from upstream: `braa public@$KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST:161:.1.3.6.*`; `braa $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST-$KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST:.$KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST.$KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST.0`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: braa - Mass SNMP scanner.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: braa - Mass SNMP scanner.
